item.title: My Favorite Cookware for Water-Based Cooking The cookware you use might matter m...
item.description:
@drmichelledavenport

My Favorite Cookware for Water-Based Cooking

The cookware you use might matter more than you think, and not for the reasons most cooking sites tell you, but because cooking the wrong way can actively work against your health.

If you’ve been here before you already know I’m all about water-based cooking, which based on countless clinical studies and research, is the best way to slow aging in the kitchen. Water-based cooking minimizes the formation of advanced glycation end products (AGEs) and other inflammatory compounds that form when we cook with high heat and oil.

The how is something I demonstrate in my videos, but the question I get most often isn’t about technique, but cookware.

Over the years, I’ve received countless questions about the cookware that appears in my videos—where to buy it, what to prioritize, whether specific pieces are necessary. The truth is, I collect (ahem, hoard) beautiful cookware and serveware. Many pieces come from travels or are sentimental objects handed down to me. The wooden bowls you often see, for example, were made by my father-in-law. I have a ceramic tea set my late grandfather handed down to me. But you don’t need any of these specific items to cook this way.

My favorite non/toxic pieces are:

Clay pots (donabes, tagines, dulsots, etc)
Stainless steel pots
Steamers and steamer inserts
Cast iron
A select number of appliances like induction pots and instant pots

For a breakdown of all these different pieces in further detail you can check out my Substack ❤️ if you want to see specific examples you can see them in my Amazon store in my link in bio too.

Xo
Dr. Michelle
.
.
.
.
.
.
#longevity #glycation #waterbasedcooking
item.content:encoded:
@drmichelledavenport

My Favorite Cookware for Water-Based Cooking

The cookware you use might matter more than you think, and not for the reasons most cooking sites tell you, but because cooking the wrong way can actively work against your health.

If you’ve been here before you already know I’m all about water-based cooking, which based on countless clinical studies and research, is the best way to slow aging in the kitchen. Water-based cooking minimizes the formation of advanced glycation end products (AGEs) and other inflammatory compounds that form when we cook with high heat and oil.

The how is something I demonstrate in my videos, but the question I get most often isn’t about technique, but cookware.

Over the years, I’ve received countless questions about the cookware that appears in my videos—where to buy it, what to prioritize, whether specific pieces are necessary. The truth is, I collect (ahem, hoard) beautiful cookware and serveware. Many pieces come from travels or are sentimental objects handed down to me. The wooden bowls you often see, for example, were made by my father-in-law. I have a ceramic tea set my late grandfather handed down to me. But you don’t need any of these specific items to cook this way.

My favorite non/toxic pieces are:

Clay pots (donabes, tagines, dulsots, etc)
Stainless steel pots
Steamers and steamer inserts
Cast iron
A select number of appliances like induction pots and instant pots

For a breakdown of all these different pieces in further detail you can check out my Substack ❤️ if you want to see specific examples you can see them in my Amazon store in my link in bio too.

Xo
Dr. Michelle
.
.
.
.
.
.
#longevity #glycation #waterbasedcooking
item.author: drmichelledavenport

item.title: The key to health and longevity is living like you’re human. Last week I stumble...
item.description:
@drmichelledavenport

The key to health and longevity is living like you’re human.

Last week I stumbled upon a video of this adorable old monk who was sharing ancient health tips. He had millions of followers but something looked off so I did some digging and that’s when I realized damn, I just got fooled by AI.

I felt so upset. Like what do you mean we are already in dystopia??

What does this mean for trust, the distribution of knowledge and human faces?

But then I calmed down and thought … an AI will never be able to share old family recipes. Or be able to show you the imperfect beauty of hand made cabbage rolls, the first dish I learned from my mom. Or reassure you by saying hey, I did my PhD only to learn that grandma was right.

Because the most human thing about our food has nothing to do with the food, you know? It’s about us.

Anyway these are cabbage rolls I made with my hands. For you to view because I care about you and your health. It’s the first dish I ever learned 20 years ago as a college student and I still love them today (thanks, Mom)!

Slow aging because they’re steamed, which you probably already know if you’ve been here!

Ingredients
1.5 lbs ground pork, chicken or broken firm tofu
A knob of ginger, grated
1 large clove of garlic, grated
1 shallot, diced finely
Splash of mirin wine
Heavy sprinkle of salt
Ground pepper if you like it
Prettiest Napa cabbage you can find, medium size
Either long chives or scallions

Directions
1. Wash then either steam (1-2 min) or microwave (2 min) the cabbage leaves until they are soft.
2. Mix the ground pork (or chicken, or tofu, whatever you are using) with the Mirin wine, salt, shallot, garlic, and ginger.
3. Place about one tablespoon of the mixture on the leaf and roll up to the stem.
4. Wrap with chives or scallions. If you are using scallions, I recommend also steaming or microwaving them so they soften.
5. Steam the rolls until the inside is cooked through, about 10-12 minutes on medium-high heat.
6. Serve with rice or on anything else you want ❤️

Enjoy!
Xo
Dr. Michelle
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
#longevitydiet #waterbasedcooking #advancedglycationendproducts #glycation
item.content:encoded:
@drmichelledavenport

The key to health and longevity is living like you’re human.

Last week I stumbled upon a video of this adorable old monk who was sharing ancient health tips. He had millions of followers but something looked off so I did some digging and that’s when I realized damn, I just got fooled by AI.

I felt so upset. Like what do you mean we are already in dystopia??

What does this mean for trust, the distribution of knowledge and human faces?

But then I calmed down and thought … an AI will never be able to share old family recipes. Or be able to show you the imperfect beauty of hand made cabbage rolls, the first dish I learned from my mom. Or reassure you by saying hey, I did my PhD only to learn that grandma was right.

Because the most human thing about our food has nothing to do with the food, you know? It’s about us.

Anyway these are cabbage rolls I made with my hands. For you to view because I care about you and your health. It’s the first dish I ever learned 20 years ago as a college student and I still love them today (thanks, Mom)!

Slow aging because they’re steamed, which you probably already know if you’ve been here!

Ingredients
1.5 lbs ground pork, chicken or broken firm tofu
A knob of ginger, grated
1 large clove of garlic, grated
1 shallot, diced finely
Splash of mirin wine
Heavy sprinkle of salt
Ground pepper if you like it
Prettiest Napa cabbage you can find, medium size
Either long chives or scallions

Directions
1. Wash then either steam (1-2 min) or microwave (2 min) the cabbage leaves until they are soft.
2. Mix the ground pork (or chicken, or tofu, whatever you are using) with the Mirin wine, salt, shallot, garlic, and ginger.
3. Place about one tablespoon of the mixture on the leaf and roll up to the stem.
4. Wrap with chives or scallions. If you are using scallions, I recommend also steaming or microwaving them so they soften.
5. Steam the rolls until the inside is cooked through, about 10-12 minutes on medium-high heat.
6. Serve with rice or on anything else you want ❤️

Enjoy!
Xo
Dr. Michelle
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
#longevitydiet #waterbasedcooking #advancedglycationendproducts #glycation
item.author: drmichelledavenport

item.title: The secret to skincare isn’t on your skin - it’s literally in your hands. Your h...
item.description:
@drmichelledavenport

The secret to skincare isn’t on your skin - it’s literally in your hands.

Your hands were meant for so much more than swiping on a screen. I’m not sure if you know this, but those beautiful hands hold everything you need to lead a healthy life. And yes, to age slowly too.

You can cook your way to slow aging, to more resilient cells, a healthy heart and flexible joints.

You can choose fresh vegetables, lift weights, open the door for a walk in the sun, call someone you love.

You can hold someone you love - yes, hugs! which we know are incomparable to any drug in promoting longevity.

You can do so much with your beautiful hands.

xo
Dr. Michelle
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
#longevitydiet #glycation #advancedglycationendproducts #waterbasedcooking
item.content:encoded:
@drmichelledavenport

The secret to skincare isn’t on your skin - it’s literally in your hands.

Your hands were meant for so much more than swiping on a screen. I’m not sure if you know this, but those beautiful hands hold everything you need to lead a healthy life. And yes, to age slowly too.

You can cook your way to slow aging, to more resilient cells, a healthy heart and flexible joints.

You can choose fresh vegetables, lift weights, open the door for a walk in the sun, call someone you love.

You can hold someone you love - yes, hugs! which we know are incomparable to any drug in promoting longevity.

You can do so much with your beautiful hands.

xo
Dr. Michelle
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
#longevitydiet #glycation #advancedglycationendproducts #waterbasedcooking
